{{< block "grid-1" >}}
{{< column "mt-2 pt-1">}}


# NeacSafe64.sys ![:inline](/images/twitter_verified.png) 

### Description

NetEase NeacSafe64.sys is an anti-cheat mini-filter driver referenced by KDU and public NeacController research. Versions prior to 1.0.0.8 expose IOCTL/message-handler paths that provide arbitrary kernel read/write primitives and can be chained for SYSTEM privilege escalation or kernel-mode code execution.
- **UUID**: d474d8ad-14d9-4664-addd-86b4a9b7a1a5
- **Created**: 2026-06-16
- **Author**: Michael Haag
- **Acknowledgement**: smallzhong | [@smallzhong](https://twitter.com/@smallzhong)

{{< button "https://github.com/magicsword-io/LOLDrivers/raw/main/drivers/dcc288fafad0bf3b505c4b44791d423d.bin" "Download" >}}
{{< tip "warning" >}}
This download link contains the vulnerable driver!

{{< /tip >}}

{{< blockbanner "NeacSafe64.sys" >}}
### Commands

```
sc.exe create NeacSafe64 binPath=C:\windows\temp\NeacSafe64.sys type=kernel &amp;&amp; sc.exe start NeacSafe64
```


| Use Case | Privileges | Operating System | 
|:---- | ---- | ---- |
| Abuse arbitrary kernel read/write primitives for local privilege escalation and kernel-mode code execution. | kernel | Windows 10, Windows 11 |



### Detections


{{< block "grid-3" >}}
{{< column >}}
#### YARA 🏹
{{< details "Expand" >}}

{{< button "https://github.com/magicsword-io/LOLDrivers/blob/main/detections/yara/yara-rules_vuln_drivers_strict.yar" "Exact Match" >}}{{< tip >}}with header and size limitation{{< /tip >}}
